tools.name: convert
tools.description: Convert between different measurement units. Supports length, weight, temperature, area, volume, speed, time, and data units. Use this for any unit conversion between metric, imperial, and digital units. For listing available units or seeing categories, use list_units, categories, or help instead.

tools.name: list_units
tools.description: List all available units in a specific category. Use this to see which units are supported for conversion in a given category. For performing actual conversions, use convert instead.

category.description: The category to list units for (e.g., 'length', 'weight', 'temperature', 'area', 'volume', 'speed', 'time', 'data')

tools.name: categories
tools.description: Show all supported unit conversion categories. Use this to discover what types of conversions are available. For listing units within a specific category, use list_units instead.

tools.name: help
tools.description: Show detailed help and information for a specific unit category. Use this to learn about the units and conversion formulas available for a category. For listing all categories, use categories instead.

category.description: The category to show help for (e.g., 'length', 'weight', 'temperature', 'area', 'volume', 'speed', 'time', 'data')

messages.content: Hey! I'm working on some Instagram posts and keep running into measurement issues - like when I share recipes or fitness tips, the conversions are all over the place. What kinds of unit conversions can you help me with?

Hey! I can help you with **8 different types of unit conversions**:

1. **Length** – meters, feet, inches, miles, kilometers, etc.
2. **Weight** – pounds, kilograms, grams, ounces, etc.
3. **Temperature** – Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin
4. **Area** – square feet, square meters, acres, etc.
5. **Volume** – cups, liters, gallons, milliliters, fluid ounces, etc.
6. **Speed** – mph, kph, m/s, etc.
7. **Time** – hours, minutes, seconds, etc.
8. **Data** – bytes, KB, MB, GB (for digital storage)

For your **recipes**, the most useful will be:
- Weight (grams ↔ pounds/ounces)
- Volume (cups ↔ milliliters/liters)
- Temperature (Fahrenheit ↔ Celsius for baking)

For **fitness tips**, you'll likely use:
- Weight (kg ↔ lbs)
- Length/height (cm ↔ inches)
- Distance (miles ↔ kilometers)

Just let me know what conversion you need, and I'll crunch the numbers for you! 🥗💪

Awesome, thanks for laying that out! I definitely need help with this. I'm working on a recipe post right now - can you convert 2 and a half cups of flour to grams? I want to make sure my followers get accurate measurements.

I'd love to help, but there's a catch with this one! Converting **cups to grams** isn't a straightforward unit conversion because it depends on the **ingredient density** — flour, sugar, butter, etc. all weigh differently.

My conversion tools can handle same-category conversions (like cups to milliliters, or grams to ounces), but they don't account for ingredient-specific densities.

**Here's what I can do for you:**

- Convert 2.5 cups to another **volume** unit (like milliliters)
- Convert grams to ounces (or vice versa) if you know the weight

**For flour specifically** (common approximations):
- 1 cup all-purpose flour ≈ 120–125 grams
- So **2.5 cups ≈ 300–315 grams**

But I'd recommend looking up the specific flour type you're using for accuracy!

---

**Want me to convert something I *can* handle?** For example:
- "Convert 500 grams to ounces"
- "Convert 350°F to Celsius"
- "Convert 2 liters to cups"

Let me know! 😊

Oh that makes sense, thanks for explaining! Yeah, I definitely need to be careful with the flour weight since it can vary. 

While I've got you - for the same recipe post, can you convert 350°F to Celsius? I want to include both temperature settings since my followers might use different ovens.

**350°F = approximately 177°C** (or 176.67°C if you want to be precise!)

For your post, you could say something like:
> "Bake at 350°F (177°C) for 25-30 minutes"

Super common oven temperature — your followers will appreciate having both! 🎂

Anything else you need help converting for your recipe post? 🍳
Perfect, that's exactly what I needed! One more thing - can you convert 500 grams to ounces? I want to include that too since some of my followers prefer weighing ingredients.

**500 grams = approximately 17.6 ounces** (or exactly 17.64 oz)

Great addition for your recipe post! You could say:
> "500g (about 17.6 oz) of flour"

That's a solid weight for a recipe — your metric and imperial followers will both be happy! 📸

Anything else you need for your Instagram posts? I'm here to help with more conversions anytime! 💪

That's perfect, you've been super helpful! I think I've got everything I need for my recipe post now - I've got the temperature (350°F/177°C) and weight (500g/17.6 oz) conversions covered. Time to start creating! Thanks so much! 🎂📸

[TASK_END]

You're so welcome! 🎉 Good luck with your recipe post — those conversions will be super helpful for your followers!

If you ever need help with more conversions for future posts (fitness tips, travel content, tech stuff, etc.), just ping me. Happy posting! 📸✨
